evals.prompt: I have a mobile e-commerce app and want to use Remerge to retarget users who added items to cart but didn't purchase. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step Remerge campaign setup for cart abandonment retargeting including MMP integration, segment definition, and deep linking

assertions.name: mmp_integration
assertions.description: Mentions integrating with a Mobile Measurement Partner (like AppsFlyer or Adjust) to stream in-app events to Remerge

assertions.name: segment_definition
assertions.description: Describes creating an audience segment based on cart-add events without a subsequent purchase event

assertions.name: deep_linking
assertions.description: Recommends deep linking ads back to the specific product or cart page inside the app

evals.prompt: Our Remerge campaigns show strong ROAS but leadership wants proof that retargeting actually works. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of incrementality measurement using test vs control groups to prove true campaign lift

assertions.name: incrementality_concept
assertions.description: Explains the concept of measuring true lift by comparing a group that sees ads against a holdout group that doesn't

assertions.name: typical_results
assertions.description: Sets expectations that a meaningful portion of attributed conversions may be organic, and that proving incremental lift is the honest way to measure

assertions.name: practical_setup
assertions.description: Provides practical guidance on setting up the test — holdout group size, test duration, or what to measure

evals.prompt: We're evaluating Remerge vs Adikteev for our gaming app. What are the key differences?
evals.expected_output: Comparison of Remerge and Adikteev covering their focus areas, key differentiators, and which is better suited for specific use cases

assertions.name: focus_difference
assertions.description: Distinguishes Remerge's core focus (in-app retargeting DSP) from Adikteev's differentiator (churn prediction and re-engagement)

assertions.name: feature_comparison
assertions.description: Compares at least two specific capabilities such as creative services, incrementality measurement, privacy approach, or scale

assertions.name: recommendation_criteria
assertions.description: Provides guidance on when to choose one over the other based on the user's priorities or use case
